_meta.title: PrusaLink — native printer.state → normalized lifecycle
_meta.family: PrusaLink on Buddy (MK4/MK4S/MK3.9, MINI/MINI+, XL, Core One) + RPi Prusa-Link (MK3S+); SL1/SL1S
_meta.confidence: 🟡 source-read (from Prusa's own published OpenAPI 3.0.1 spec); hardware-unvalidated / partial

derived_from.source_note: Clean-room from the vendor's own published OpenAPI 3.0.1 spec (prusa3d/Prusa-Link-Web). No code copied; no secrets.

notes: `printer.state` comes from `GET /api/v1/status` (the `printer` block is the one required field). A job-level subset also appears as `job.state` (PRINTING/PAUSED/FINISHED/STOPPED/ERROR) from `GET /api/v1/job`.
`READY` and `ATTENTION` are near-unique states with no crisp cross-brand analogue. `READY` is distinct from `IDLE` only on Buddy firmware — collapse it to standby but keep a `ready` hint.
Map `ATTENTION` to a NEEDS-USER signal, not silently to plain `error`: the printer is waiting on a human (filament runout, MMU error, 'remove print' dialog). Its exact triggers are an open gap.
`BUSY` is a mid-operation transient (homing, loading) — treat as busy for write-gating; it is not acceptable for a new job.
Progress here is TIME-based (unlike file-byte on Klipper/OctoPrint); times are in seconds.

mappings.note: keep a `ready` hint; distinct from IDLE only on Buddy firmware
mid-operation (homing/loading); not acceptable for a new job
waiting on a human — surface as needs-user, NOT plain error
